published_prior_art.cite: LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee leaves conditional sufficient-set sharp aggregation out of scope; Dinkelbach1967 supplies only generic fractional-program machinery
JonzonGabrielSjolanderSachs2025CovariatesBounds gives the pointwise/uniform sharpness distinction; this adds a model-specific common-threshold certificate
LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee sufficient-value case identifies pi_AT(X) and yields the standard trimmed functional
ColangeloLee2025ContinuousDML and BenMichael2025ConditionalLP do not cover a denominator-coupled measurable-selector support-function endpoint
FangSantos2019DirectionalInference is generic directional-delta theory, not this Lee-type Dinkelbach endpoint map

soundness_flags.label: ass:active-set-margin
soundness_flags.one_line: A positive value gap to the best competing t over a continuum interval is generally impossible for the continuous trimmed objective; the stated density-at-zero justification supports no-tie uniqueness, not a uniform value gap.

soundness_flags.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint
soundness_flags.one_line: The asymptotic linearity claim omits the population root derivative, kernel bias/undersmoothing and local treatment-density conditions, and defines variance through the random hat_Phi object rather than a fixed influence function.

soundness_flags.label: ass:quantile-regularity / ass:nuisance-estimability / ass:bandwidth
soundness_flags.one_line: The Colangelo-Lee citation supports kernel DML for point-identified continuous-treatment ADRFs, not trimmed-quantile plus Frechet-band nuisance estimation with only h_n -> 0 and n h_n -> infinity.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:boundary-uniform-inference
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Uniform coverage at active-set ties is explicitly left open rather than claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The inferential claim is only regular-regime Wald validity after the strict-margin and kernel regularity conditions are repaired.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace ass:active-set-margin with a feasible primitive condition, such as finite endpoint-candidate separation or a curvature/anti-concentration condition that yields a valid selector-map derivative.

Rewrite prop:regular-dr-endpoint with an explicit population score, derivative -partial_lambda Phi_sigma(lambda_sigma^*;P), treatment-density positivity, kernel order, and undersmoothing/bias conditions.
Add a small two-covariate worked example where pointwise Frechet plug-in fails the common-threshold condition, so the global selector object is visibly non-routine.
Move the nuisance-estimability citation burden from Colangelo-Lee alone to LeeLiu/Semenova for trimmed quantiles and state separately which Frechet-band nuisances are estimated and at what norm/rate.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle; the identification novelty is field-level, but the regular-inference layer and active-set condition need semantic repair before Stage 0.
